description:
You are a platform engineer reviewing configuration files that control a legacy CI/CD
    pipeline.  
    The repository clone mounted in the container already contains an INI-style
    configuration file located at

        /home/user/projects/ci_cd/config/build_settings.ini

    What you need to do:

    1. Parse the INI file and read  
       • the key “python_version” inside the [runtime] section, and  
       • the key “timeout”         inside the [build]   section.  

    2. Create a **new** plain-text log file

        /home/user/projects/ci_cd/logs/config_summary.log

       The file must contain exactly two lines, in the order shown below,
       terminated by a single UNIX newline at the end of the second line:

           python_version=<value_taken_from_ini>
           timeout=<value_taken_from_ini>

       No extra blank lines or spaces are allowed anywhere in the file.

    3. Print the two extracted lines to STDOUT in the same order and format
       as they appear in the log file so the pipeline console shows them.

    Additional requirements & hints:

    • You may assume the configuration file already exists and is readable.  
    • If the directory “/home/user/projects/ci_cd/logs” does not yet exist,
      create it with default user permissions so the log file can be written.
    • Solve the entire task with a single terminal command (pipelines using pipes,
      subshells, redirections, etc. are permitted; avoid running multiple full
      commands separated by “&&” or “;”).
    • Do not modify the original INI file; only read from it.
    • Your solution is considered correct when
        – the log file exists at the exact path above,  
        – its content matches the strict two-line format described,  
        – and the identical two lines are echoed to the terminal.
truth:
Pre-placed files and their contents at task start:

    1. Path: /home/user/projects/ci_cd/config/build_settings.ini  
       Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)  
       Content (with trailing newline):

           [build]
           artifact_name=webapp
           timeout=420
           retries=3

           [runtime]
           python_version=3.10
           entry_point=app:main

           [notifications]
           email=devops@example.com

    2. Directory /home/user/projects/ci_cd/logs does NOT exist initially.

    Files/Artifacts expected after the agent finishes:

    1. Path: /home/user/projects/ci_cd/logs/config_summary.log  
       Permissions: 0644 (rw-r--r--)  
       Content (with trailing newline):

           python_version=3.10
           timeout=420

    2. The agent must also output exactly:

           python_version=3.10
           timeout=420

       to STDOUT (followed by a newline after the second line).

    The automated grader will:

       • Confirm that the log file exists at the specified location.  
       • Compare its exact byte content to the expected two-line text above.  
       • Verify that the agent’s console output matches the same two-line string.
